title: Autocomplete label-interface unification

queries: graph llm classification train inference mismatch label interface candidate scoring
graph rag candidate ranking interface structured prediction label verbalizer mismatch

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: The graph-to-LLM interface is a central bottleneck, so failures at the prompt/output interface can dominate over encoder-side improvements.
papers.relevance: Supports treating the multiclass label interface itself as the next structural bottleneck after repeated upstream partial wins.

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: Downstream performance depends on exposing graph evidence in a form that the language model can consume consistently.
papers.relevance: Supports unifying the train-time and inference-time candidate interface rather than keeping separate prompt-to-label and prompt-to-token objectives.

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: Graph-enhanced generation systems must align graph evidence organization with downstream consumption and decision interfaces.
papers.relevance: Supports a candidate-aware label-interface redesign for autocomplete-style multiclass ranking tasks.

evidence.why_now: EXP078 was the first recent Stage 3 bundle to improve both user-churn and user-ltv while still failing on item-incoterms. Code inspection after that bundle shows a structural mismatch specific to multiclass autocomplete tasks: the active direct-supervision path optimizes integer class ids against label embeddings built from a separate raw-label verbalizer, while the codebase also contains a class-id candidate-scoring path.

hypothesis: The persistent item-incoterms regression is not only an evidence-retrieval or alignment problem. In the active autocomplete multiclass path, direct supervision uses integer class-id targets but the label scorer constructs class prototypes from a separate raw-label verbalizer such as `target_col: raw_label`. Unifying that scorer around the same class-id interface used by the target labels should reduce salt-side ranking mismatch without revisiting saturated loss or sampling families.
notes: This candidate is now wired end-to-end and should be evaluated as a full representative-task bundle, even though the new label-interface path only activates on autocomplete-style multiclass tasks.
The key active-path mismatch is in model.py: autocomplete multiclass direct supervision uses sample_head vs label_head over `get_label_texts(...)`, while the targets themselves are integer class ids.
This candidate should stay finetune-only and should not reintroduce pretrain, route-aware loss, bridge-route sampling, or basis-gating scans.
Launched on 2026-05-07 after syncing the updated main.py/model.py/utils.py label-interface patch plus generated wrappers and launcher to the remote repo and /tmp.
Selected currently idle GPUs at launch time: user-churn on GPU 0, user-ltv on GPU 5, item-incoterms on GPU 6.
First orchestrator status check shows all three windows up and logs at remote DB-load stage.
Completed on 2026-05-07 with global verdict: failed.

The class-id label-interface unification reduced the salt-side gap versus the strict baseline, but item-incoterms still remained below the MRR gate and user-ltv regressed enough to invalidate the bundle.
